display.purpose: Hero H1 only, one per page.

headline.purpose: Page-level H1/H2 — course and lesson titles.

title.purpose: Card and section-level H2/H3, stat numbers.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, badges, stat tags — DM Mono only.

shadows.name: ambient-card
shadows.purpose: Resting state for course cards, stat tiles, practice cards.

shadows.name: lifted-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover state on cards and lesson-list rows.

shadows.name: deep-panel
shadows.purpose: The schedule calendar and other large, prominent glass surfaces.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover lifts, presses, and transitions.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Card hover, border-color, and shadow transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Gold gradient CTA — start a course, mark a lesson complete.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: linear-gradient(180deg, #f8cb85 0%, #f0b155 14%, #e6a03c 55%, #d38f2e 100%); color: #0f2c52; border: 0; border-radius: 5px; padding: 14px 18px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'DM Sans', Arial, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.65) inset, 0 5px 14px -4px rgba(179,110,20,0.6); transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); box-shadow: 0 2px 5px -2px rgba(179,110,20,0.5) inset; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f2c52; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Header Button
components.description: Navy gradient CTA reserved for the persistent nav header.
components.css: .ds-btn-header { background: linear-gradient(180deg, #2a4d82 0%, #17386b 14%, #0f2c52 55%, #0b2242 100%); color: #fff; border: 0; border-radius: 5px; padding: 11px 16px; font-weight: 700; font-size: 14px; font-family: 'DM Sans', Arial, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.18) inset, 0 5px 14px -4px rgba(5,21,38,0.55); transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-header:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-header:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: Lit Glass surface used for course cards, stat tiles, and practice cards.
components.html:
Spam Calls & Messages

Learn to spot the patterns scammers rely on.
components.css: .ds-card-glass { background: rgba(219,232,246,0.6); backdrop-filter: blur(12px) saturate(160%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px) saturate(160%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.7); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.5) inset, 0 8px 20px -14px rgba(15,44,82,0.35); transition: transform 220ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card-glass:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.4) inset, 0 12px 24px -14px rgba(15,44,82,0.3); } .ds-card-title { font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 24px; margin: 0 0 10px; color: #0a1f33; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'DM Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; color: #52697f; margin: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input used across auth, admin, and contact forms.
components.css: .ds-input { padding: 10px 12px; border: 1px solid #ccd8e4; border-radius: 5px; font-size: 15px; font-family: 'DM Sans', Arial, sans-serif; background: #fff; color: #0a1f33; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 3px rgba(10,31,51,0.1); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f2c52; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Badge Pill
components.description: Metadata pill — week numbers, XP/streak counts, example tags. Never a call to action.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; color: #0f2c52; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #fff 0%, #e6edf5 100%); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.7) inset; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 999px; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Quiz Choice Button
components.description: The core stateful component — a quiz answer that behaves like a button but reads as a card. States: default, hover, selected, correct, incorrect, dimmed.
components.html: <button class="ds-choice ds-choice--correct">A stranger calling and asking to verify your bank PIN</button>
components.css: .ds-choice { display: block; width: 100%; text-align: left; padding: 16px 18px; border: 2px solid #ccd8e4; border-radius: 8px; background: #fff; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8) inset, 0 2px 6px -4px rgba(15,44,82,0.2); font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', Arial, sans-serif; color: #0a1f33; transition: 150ms border-color, 150ms background, 150ms box-shadow, transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-choice:hover:not(:disabled) { border-color: #1f6fb2; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-choice--correct { border-color: #2e8540; background: #e3f3e6; color: #1c5c2b; } .ds-choice--incorrect { border-color: #b3261e; background: #fbe4e2; color: #7a1a15; } .ds-choice--dimmed { opacity: 0.55; }

narrative.overview: VeraBlock's visual world is a deep, steady navy harbor with a warm gold lantern cutting through it — trustworthy and unhurried on one hand, warm and encouraging on the other. Surfaces are built from Lit Glass: translucent panels that blur and saturate what's behind them, each catching a thin highlight along its top edge like light on the water. A faint paper grain runs under every page, so even the sharpest gradients still feel handmade rather than manufactured on a screen. This system explicitly rejects the corporate cybersecurity vendor look — no fear-based dark UI, no generic enterprise navy-and-lock-icon SaaS aesthetic, no manufactured urgency. It equally rejects childish edtech — no cartoon mascots, no cutesy illustration, nothing that risks reading as patronizing to an adult, senior-heavy audience.
keyCharacteristics: Navy-and-gold palette carrying civic trust and warm encouragement in the same breath
Lit Glass surfaces: translucent, blurred, saturated, edge-lit — never flat opaque cards
Serif display type (Fraunces) paired with a plain, humanist sans body (DM Sans) and a mono label voice (DM Mono)
Soft, tinted shadows instead of neutral gray ones — blue-tinted under navy/paper surfaces, gold-tinted under gold/gamified surfaces
Generous touch targets, honored prefers-reduced-motion, and text sized for a senior-heavy audience

rules.name: The Tinted Shadow Rule
rules.body: No shadow is neutral gray. Shadows under blue/navy/paper surfaces carry rgba(15,44,82,…); shadows under gold/gamified surfaces carry rgba(179,110,20,…). A gray drop shadow anywhere in this system is a bug.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Gold means action or achievement — a button, a completed step, a streak. It never appears as decoration. If gold shows up and nothing is being asked of or credited to the user, remove it.

rules.name: The Serif-Says-Trust Rule
rules.body: Fraunces appears only on headings, blockquotes, and the large numbers in stat tiles — never on body copy, buttons, or labels.

rules.name: The No-Tiny-Type Rule
rules.body: Nothing below 11px, and body text never drops below 1rem (16px) even in dense admin/practice UI. Treat 16px as the floor given the senior-heavy audience.

rules.name: The Inset Highlight Rule
rules.body: Every elevated surface gets a thin white inset highlight along its top edge before anything else — it's what separates glass catching light from a flat card with a shadow.

dos: Do use the Lit Glass treatment (inset highlight + tinted shadow + blur/saturate backdrop) on every elevated surface — it's the system's signature, not an occasional flourish.
Do keep gold reserved for action and achievement (buttons, completed states, streaks).
Do hold body text at 16px minimum and give muted/secondary text real contrast against paper — this audience skews senior.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion on every hover/press/entrance animation.
Do keep quiz and form states distinguishable by border/shape, not color alone.
donts: Don't design anything that reads as a corporate cybersecurity vendor — no fear-based dark UI, no generic enterprise navy-and-lock-icon SaaS look, no manufactured urgency copy.
Don't design anything that reads as childish edtech — no cartoon mascots, no cutesy illustration style.
Don't use a flat, untinted gray box-shadow anywhere — every shadow in this system is tinted navy or gold.
Don't skip the inset highlight on a card and call it done.
Don't drop label type (DM Mono) below 11px or use it for anything longer than a short tag/number.
